description: buffer overflow vulnerability
heap buffer overflow due to incorrect size calculation
stack buffer overflow from unbounded copy into fixed-size local array
use-after-free vulnerability where freed memory is subsequently accessed
double free where the same memory allocation is freed twice
memory leak where allocated memory is never freed on certain code paths
null pointer dereference due to missing NULL check before access
read of uninitialized memory leading to undefined behavior
race condition on shared data accessed from multiple threads without synchronization
deadlock caused by inconsistent lock acquisition ordering
missing mutex lock around critical section accessing shared state
integer overflow leading to incorrect computation or wraparound
signed/unsigned integer comparison mismatch leading to incorrect branch
integer truncation when assigning a wider type to a narrower type
off-by-one error in array indexing or loop boundary
missing break statement in switch case causing unintended fallthrough
incorrect loop bounds causing iteration over invalid indices
missing return statement in non-void function on certain code paths
incorrect type cast causing data corruption or misinterpretation
unchecked return value from a function that can indicate failure
missing error propagation where a failure is silently ignored
missing NULL check after memory allocation that can fail
file descriptor leak where an opened fd is not closed on error paths
missing cleanup of allocated resources on early return or error path
incorrect argument order in function call swapping similarly-typed parameters
string buffer overrun from unbounded string copy or concatenation
missing null terminator on a string buffer leading to out-of-bounds read
copy-paste error where duplicated code was not correctly updated for its new context
hardware register misconfiguration writing wrong value to a peripheral control register
incorrect interrupt priority or missing interrupt enable causing missed or nested interrupt issues
DMA buffer not aligned to required boundary causing transfer corruption
peripheral initialization in wrong order causing dependent hardware to malfunction
incorrect clock divider or PLL configuration leading to wrong peripheral frequency
GPIO pin multiplexing conflict where same pin is configured for two different functions
watchdog timer not serviced on a long-running code path causing unexpected reset
missing volatile qualifier on hardware register access causing compiler to optimize away reads
byte order error when reading multi-byte value from hardware register or network buffer
timer counter overflow not handled causing time calculation error
RTOS task stack size too small causing stack overflow into adjacent memory
priority inversion where high-priority task is blocked by lower-priority task holding a shared resource
semaphore or mutex not released on error path causing eventual resource starvation
blocking RTOS API called from interrupt context causing undefined behavior
incorrect protocol state machine transition violating specification
missing timeout handling on a protocol wait causing indefinite hang
incorrect message parsing or decoding leading to corrupted data extraction
